gold_final_answer: All solutions are f(z) = c z for all z, where c is a complex constant with |c| = 1.
prompt: Determine all functions $f: \mathbb{C} \to \mathbb{C}$ such that $|wf(z) + zf(w)| = 2|zw|$, for any $z, w \in \mathbb{C}$.
response_text:
We seek all functions \(f:\mathbb C\to\mathbb C\) such that for all \(z,w\in\mathbb C\),
\[
|w f(z)+ z f(w)|=2|zw|.
\]
We will show the only such functions are
\[
f(z)=z \quad\text{for all }z,
\qquad\text{or}\qquad
f(z)=-z \quad\text{for all }z.
\]

## Step 1: Determine \(f(0)\)

Set \(w=0\). Then
\[
|0\cdot f(z)+ z f(0)|=0,
\]
so
\[
|z f(0)|=0 \quad \forall z.
\]
Taking \(z\neq 0\), we get \(f(0)=0\).

---

## Step 2: For \(z\neq 0\), \(|f(z)|=|z|\)

Set \(w=z\). Then
\[
|z f(z)+ z f(z)|=2|z|^2,
\]
so
\[
|2 z f(z)|=2|z|^2.
\]
Hence
\[
|f(z)|=|z| \qquad (\forall z\neq 0).
\]
Together with \(f(0)=0\), this means
\[
|f(z)|=|z| \quad \forall z\in\mathbb C.
\]

So for each \(z\neq 0\), there exists \(u(z)\) with \(|u(z)|=1\) such that
\[
f(z)=u(z)z.
\]

---

## Step 3: Rewrite the condition

For nonzero \(z,w\), write
\[
f(z)=u(z)z,\qquad f(w)=u(w)w,\qquad |u(z)|=|u(w)|=1.
\]
Then
\[
w f(z)+ z f(w)=w(u(z)z)+z(u(w)w)=zw\,(u(z)+u(w)).
\]
So the given equation becomes
\[
|zw|\,|u(z)+u(w)|=2|zw|.
\]
If \(zw\neq 0\), divide by \(|zw|\):
\[
|u(z)+u(w)|=2 \qquad \forall z,w\neq 0.
\]

Now for complex numbers of modulus \(1\), we have
\[
|a+b|\le |a|+|b|=2,
\]
with equality iff \(a\) and \(b\) have the same argument, i.e. \(a=b\).

Thus from \(|u(z)+u(w)|=2\), we get
\[
u(z)=u(w)\qquad \forall z,w\neq 0.
\]
Hence \(u\) is constant on \(\mathbb C^\times\): there exists a fixed \(c\) with \(|c|=1\) such that
\[
f(z)=cz \qquad \forall z\neq 0.
\]
Also \(f(0)=0\), so actually
\[
f(z)=cz \qquad \forall z\in\mathbb C.
\]

---

## Step 4: Determine \(c\)

Substitute \(f(z)=cz\) into the original condition:
\[
|w(cz)+z(cw)|=|2czw|=2|c||zw|=2|zw|.
\]
This only requires \(|c|=1\), but we must check carefully: since
\[
wf(z)+zf(w)=2czw,
\]
the condition is indeed satisfied for every unit complex \(c\).

Wait — this seems to give all \(f(z)=cz\) with \(|c|=1\). Let us verify:
\[
|wf(z)+zf(w)|=|w(cz)+z(cw)|=|2czw|=2|zw|.
\]
Yes, this works.

So all such functions are exactly
\[
f(z)=cz \quad \text{for some constant } c\in\mathbb C,\ |c|=1.
\]

---

Final answer: \(\boxed{f(z)=cz\ \text{for all }z\in\mathbb C,\ \text{where }|c|=1}\).
